issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger buttons lack aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking or pressing Enter/Space on a dropdown trigger opens or closes the panel, but the code never updates aria‑expanded on the trigger element. Screen readers receive no state change.
issuesFound.fix:
In both _onTriggerClick and _onTriggerKeydown, after _openInstance/closeInstance, add:  
```js
setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(inst.open));
```  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open() does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is opened, the code adds CSS classes and animates the panel but never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the question button. The button’s aria‑expanded remains false, misleading AT users.
issuesFound.fix:
After opening, add:  
```js
setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Period‑selection buttons lack aria‑pressed (state)
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period buttons (`data-period`) toggle between “month”, “quarter”, and “year” but never set aria‑pressed to indicate which period is active. Keyboard users cannot tell the current selection.
issuesFound.fix:
After determining the active button, set:  
```js
setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn === activeBtn));
```

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav toggle button is a two‑state control without aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button toggles the navigation drawer but never updates aria‑pressed, so its pressed state is not exposed to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Inside toggle(), after changing _open, add:  
```js
setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', String(_open));
```

issuesFound.title: Site‑search result count is not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: After fetching suggestions or showing “no results”, the code updates a hidden status element’s text but the element is not aria‑live. Users of screen readers receive no audible feedback.
issuesFound.fix:
When creating _statusEl, set `aria-live="polite"` (and optionally `role="status"`), or call `liveRegion.announce()` instead of merely changing text.  

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The badge showing the number of visible products is updated visually but no live‑region announcement is made, leaving AT users unaware of filter results.
issuesFound.fix:
After setting the badge text, call:  
```js
liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count===1?'':'s'} shown`);
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on error fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field fails validation, the code adds an error message element but never adds `aria-invalid="true"` to the input, so assistive technologies cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
In the error‑render path, add:  
```js
field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not clear aria‑invalid when the field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: On successful validation the code removes the error message but does not remove or set `aria-invalid="false"` on the field, leaving a stale invalid state for AT.
issuesFound.fix:
In the success branch, add:  
```js
field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav trigger lacks initial aria‑expanded value
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button is created without an explicit `aria-expanded="false"` on page load, violating the requirement that toggle widgets expose their initial state.
issuesFound.fix:
In init(), after locating _trigger, add:  
```js
setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu triggers lack initial aria‑expanded value
issuesFound.explanation: Each dropdown trigger button is given `aria-haspopup="true"` but never receives an initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, so screen readers cannot know the default collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix:
After creating each inst, add:  
```js
setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav drawer is hidden via CSS classes without aria‑hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is hidden by removing the `is-open` class, but the element remains in the accessibility tree because no `aria-hidden="true"` is applied. AT users can still navigate to hidden navigation links.
issuesFound.fix:
In close(), after removing the open class, add:  
```js
drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');
```  
and in open() remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce the new billing period
issuesFound.explanation: When the user switches billing periods, the UI updates prices but no live‑region announcement is made, so screen‑reader users are unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating the UI, add:  
```js
liveRegion.announce(`Billing period switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`);
```

issuesFound.title: View‑mode toggle does not announce the new layout
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between grid and list view updates CSS classes but provides no audible feedback for AT users.
issuesFound.fix:
After applying the class, add:  
```js
liveRegion.announce(`View mode changed to ${mode} view`);
```

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestion list is shown/hidden and its content changes, but no dedicated live‑region is updated for each change, violating the requirement to announce dynamic UI updates.
issuesFound.fix:
After rendering suggestions, call:  
```js
liveRegion.announce(`${suggestions.length} suggestion${suggestions.length===1?'':'s'} available`);
```

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` function toggles the navigation drawer but never sets `aria‑expanded` on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening and `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` when closing.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close function does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.close()` function hides the drawer without resetting `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button, leaving the state stale for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` when the drawer is closed.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The `open(item, …)` function expands a FAQ panel but only sets `aria‑expanded` to `false` in `close()`. When a panel is opened, `aria‑expanded` remains `false`, misleading assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening the panel.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close function correctly sets aria‑expanded to false (no issue) – (included for completeness).
Pricing period buttons lack aria‑pressed to reflect state
issuesFound.explanation: The period toggle buttons (`[data-period]`) receive a visual `active` class but never receive `aria‑pressed` updates, so screen‑reader users cannot tell which billing period is active.
issuesFound.fix: In `_syncToggleState()` add `setAttr(btn, 'aria‑pressed', isActive ? 'true' : 'false');` for each button.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle (simple switch) does not set aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off toggle (`[data-billing-toggle]`) changes visual state but never updates `aria‑pressed`, leaving its state ambiguous to AT.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, call `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria‑pressed', isYear ? 'true' : 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator` marks fields as invalid visually but never adds `aria‑invalid="true"` or removes it on valid input, violating ARIA best practice for form validation.
issuesFound.fix: In `_renderResult()` when `!result.valid` call `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` and when valid `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');`.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid to false on valid fields (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: As above, when a field becomes valid the validator removes visual error classes but does not explicitly clear `aria‑invalid`, leaving a stale invalid state for AT.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` is called when the field is valid.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria-controls linking to drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) controls the navigation drawer (`_drawer`) but lacks an `aria-controls` attribute referencing the drawer’s `id`, breaking the relationship for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Assign an `id` to the drawer (if missing) and set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', drawerId);`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria-controls linking to panel
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ question buttons have `aria-controls` set via `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId)`, but the panel element may lack a matching `id` if none existed, causing a broken reference.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure each panel has an `id` (e.g., via `srUtils.ensureId(panel, 'faq-panel')`) before assigning `aria-controls`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons missing aria-controls linking to associated content
issuesFound.explanation: Period toggle buttons control price display elements but do not have `aria-controls` pointing to the region they affect, reducing discoverability for AT.
issuesFound.fix: Give each price display container an `id` and set `setAttr(btn, 'aria-controls', panelId);`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panel does not set aria-hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is closed, the code removes the `open` class but does not set `aria-hidden="true"` on the panel, so the element remains perceivable to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In `_closeInstance()` add `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` when closing, and remove it when opening.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer does not set aria-hidden when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation drawer is hidden via CSS classes but never receives `aria-hidden="true"` when closed, leaving it in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In `close()` add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` and in `open()` remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel does not set aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ answer panel is collapsed, the code sets `hidden` attribute but does not set `aria-hidden="true"` explicitly, which may be required for some AT implementations.
issuesFound.fix: In `close()` add `setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` and remove it in `open()`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter “view‑mode” toggle button does not set aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The button that switches between grid and list view toggles a CSS class but never updates `aria-pressed`, leaving its state ambiguous for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', mode === 'list' ? 'true' : 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing comparison table column highlight does not set aria‑selected or aria‑current
issuesFound.explanation: When a column is highlighted, the code adds a visual `featured` class but does not convey the selection to AT via `aria-selected` or `aria-current`.
issuesFound.fix: In `highlightColumn()` add `setAttr(col, 'aria-selected', 'true');` and clear it on other columns.

issuesFound.fix:

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset and border-radius as replacements. Outline-offset alone does not create a visible focus ring, and border-radius only affects corner rounding. Keyboard users will not see a focus indicator on any element that relies on this rule.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block removes the outline on :focus-visible and provides no visible alternative. In Windows High Contrast Mode, box-shadow is suppressed, so users will have no focus indicator at all.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This selector inherits outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule and only changes outline-offset to -3px. Without a visible outline, border, or box-shadow, keyboard users cannot see when inset focus ring elements receive focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. No box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline is provided as a replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when buttons receive focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link focus-visible rule removes the outline and provides no visible alternative. Keyboard users navigating to the site logo will not see a focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links remove the outline on focus-visible and only set a negative outline-offset. No visible focus indicator is provided for keyboard users navigating submenu items.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transition on transform property causing 3D rotation on hover, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 939 is empty and does not disable this motion. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons have a transition on the transform property (translateY on active), but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this spatial movement for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link

The contrast ratio is approximately 2.1:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text. Users with low vision cannot read these labels.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() functions toggle CSS classes on the navigation drawer but never update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger) to reflect the open or closed state. Screen readers cannot announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1155: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside open(). Line 1173: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside close().

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _open() and _close() functions in siteSearch show and hide the suggestion listbox but never update aria-expanded on the search input (_inputEl). Screen readers cannot announce when the suggestion list is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2453: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside _open(). Line 2461: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside _close().

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState() function toggles the active CSS class on billing period buttons (_periodBtns) but does not set aria-pressed to indicate which period is selected. Screen readers cannot identify the pressed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3255: Inside the forEach loop, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The themeSwitcher.set() function updates the theme preference but does not set aria-pressed on the theme toggle buttons ([data-theme-toggle]) to indicate which theme is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4155: Inside the set() function, after updating _current and _apply(), add `qsa('[data-theme-toggle]').forEach(btn => { setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-theme-toggle') === pref)); });`.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The _renderResult() function in inlineValidator marks fields as invalid by adding CSS classes and updating aria-describedby, but never sets aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or aria-invalid="false" on valid fields. Screen readers cannot identify invalid fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3805: In the else block (invalid), add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`. Line 3808: In the if block (valid), add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function selects the trigger button but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" when the navigation is closed on page load. Screen readers assume the state is unknown.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1208: After `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);` and the null check, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters() function updates the product count badge and shows/hides cards but does not announce the filter results to screen readers. Users are not informed that the content has changed or how many results are showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3520: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(totalMatches === 0 ? 'No products match your filters' : `Showing ${totalMatches} products`);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod() function updates prices when switching between monthly and annual billing but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed that prices have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3240: After updating the plan cards, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'month' ? STRINGS.pricingMonthly : STRINGS.pricingAnnual);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions show and hide the mobile navigation drawer but do not announce the state change to screen readers. Users are not informed that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1157: In open(), add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);`. Line 1175: In close(), add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions open and close dropdown menus but do not announce these actions. Screen reader users may not realise a dropdown has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1387: In _openInstance(), add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown opened');`. Line 1400: In _closeInstance(), add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown closed');`.

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips removal missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The _onChipClick() function removes filter chips and updates the product grid but does not announce that a filter has been removed or that the results have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3660: After calling the filter removal function (e.g., productFilter.removeTag), add `liveRegion.announce('Filter removed');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function reveals the navigation drawer but does not move keyboard focus into the drawer. Keyboard users remain on the trigger button and must tab through the entire page to reach the newly opened menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1160: After opening the drawer, add `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function identifies the trigger button and drawer elements but does not set aria-controls on the trigger to establish the relationship between the button and the drawer it controls.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1210: After ensuring _drawer has an id (e.g., `const drawerId = srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav');`), add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', drawerId);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable for focus trapping but never initialises or activates a focus trap when the menu opens. Keyboard users can tab out of the drawer into the background page content while the drawer is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1155: In open(), add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer); _trap.activate();`. Line 1170: In close(), add `if (_trap) { _trap.deactivate(); _trap = null; }`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close missing focus return
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function hides the navigation drawer but does not return keyboard focus to the trigger button that opened it. Focus is lost to the document body, forcing keyboard users to start tabbing from the beginning.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1180: At the end of close(), before or after `_unlockScroll();`, add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer toggle functions add and remove CSS classes to show or hide the drawer, but they do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the open or closed state. Screen readers will not announce the current state of the navigation menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line open(): add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` Line close(): add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu open and close functions manage the panel visibility by adding CSS classes and removing the hidden attribute, but they never set aria-expanded on the trigger button. This prevents assistive technologies from knowing whether the dropdown is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line _openInstance(): add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` Line _closeInstance(): add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open and close functions animate the panel height and manage the hidden attribute, but they do not synchronise the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button with the visible state. This violates the disclosure pattern required by WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line open(): add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` Line close(): add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle buttons missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/quarterly/year) act as a single-select button group where one button is always pressed. The code updates CSS classes to show the active state but never sets aria-pressed to communicate the pressed state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line _syncToggleState(): inside the _periodBtns.forEach loop, add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: productFilter category buttons missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The category filter tab buttons use aria-pressed to indicate the active filter, but the function only toggles CSS classes and does not update the aria-pressed attribute to reflect which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line _syncTabAriaStates(): inside the forEach loop, add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not setting aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the markInvalid function adds the error message to aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not identify the field as having an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line markInvalid(): add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` Line _renderResult() (invalid branch): add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Form validation valid state not clearing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the code removes the error styling and aria-describedby reference but leaves aria-invalid="true" set (or fails to set aria-invalid="false"). This causes screen readers to continue announcing the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line markValid(): add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` Line _renderResult() (valid branch): add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is initialised without setting aria-expanded="false" to indicate that the menu is closed by default. This prevents screen readers from knowing the initial state of the disclosure widget.
issuesFound.fix: Line init(): after `_initialised = true;`, add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have an aria-controls attribute pointing to the drawer element it controls. This prevents assistive technologies from identifying the relationship between the trigger and the navigation panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line init(): after ensuring _drawer has an id (using srUtils.ensureId), add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown, the trigger button is given aria-haspopup and aria-controls, but aria-expanded is not initialised to "false" to indicate that the menu is closed on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line register(): after setting aria-controls, add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Product filter changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the user changes product filters, the visible product count updates and cards animate, but no announcement is made to screen readers about the result count or that the filter has been applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line applyFilters(): after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(totalMatches) : `${totalMatches} products found`);`

issuesFound.title: Pricing period changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the user switches billing periods (monthly/quarterly/annual), the prices update visually and the savings message appears, but screen readers receive no announcement about the price change or the new billing period selection.
issuesFound.fix: Line setPeriod(): after `_syncToggleState(period);`, add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Billing period changed to {period}').replace('{period}', BILLING_PERIODS[period].label));`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens or closes, the visual state changes and focus is managed, but screen readers do not receive an announcement confirming that the navigation menu has opened or closed.

issuesFound.explanation: The DesktopNav component contains a nav element without an aria-label. Since there are multiple nav elements on the page, screen reader users cannot distinguish this as the main desktop navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line: DesktopNav component, nav element: Add aria-label="Main navigation" to the nav element.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The MobileNav component contains a nav element without an aria-label. Since there are multiple nav elements on the page, screen reader users cannot distinguish this as the mobile navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line: MobileNav component, nav element: Add aria-label="Mobile navigation" to the nav element.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required field marker missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The required field asterisk marker is not hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers will announce "star" or "asterisk" separately from the "(required)" text that follows, creating redundant and potentially confusing announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line: FormGroup component, required marker span: Add aria-hidden="true" to the span containing the asterisk.

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy when loading
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component displays a loading spinner when isLoading is true, but it does not set aria-busy="true" on the button element. Screen reader users are not informed that the button is currently busy processing.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Button component, button element: Add aria-busy={isLoading ? "true" : undefined} to the button props.

issuesFound.title: HeroSection landmark missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The HeroSection uses a section element containing a heading with id="hero-heading", but the section lacks aria-labelledby to expose that heading as the landmark's accessible name. Screen reader users navigating by landmarks cannot identify this as the hero section.
issuesFound.fix: Line: HeroSection component, section element: Add aria-labelledby="hero-heading" to the section props.

issuesFound.title: StatsBar landmark missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The StatsBar uses a section element containing a heading with id="stats-heading", but the section lacks aria-labelledby to expose that heading as the landmark's accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line: StatsBar component, section element: Add aria-labelledby="stats-heading" to the section props.

issuesFound.title: HeroSection trust badges group missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The trust badges container groups related content (certification badges and ratings) but lacks an aria-label to identify the purpose of this group to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: HeroSection component, trust badges div: Add aria-label="Trust indicators and certifications" to the div props.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav backdrop interactive div missing button role
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop div has an onClick handler to close the navigation but lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and keyboard event handlers. Keyboard users cannot activate this element to close the navigation, and screen reader users do not recognize it as interactive.
issuesFound.fix: Line: MobileNav component, backdrop div: Add role="button", tabIndex={0}, aria-label="Close navigation", and onKeyDown handler to support Enter and Space keys.

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay interactive div missing button role
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay div has an onClick handler to close the modal but lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and keyboard event handlers. Keyboard users cannot activate this element to close the modal.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Modal component, overlay div: Add role="button", tabIndex={0}, aria-label="Close modal", and onKeyDown handler to support Enter and Space keys.

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal misplaced on overlay instead of dialog
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay div instead of the dialog div. This incorrectly marks the overlay as the modal element rather than the dialog content, potentially causing screen readers to misidentify the modal boundaries.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Modal component, overlay div: Remove aria-modal="true" from the overlay div. Line: Modal component, dialog div: Add aria-modal="true" to the div with role="dialog".

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar cloned list missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated list of integration logos for visual marquee looping lacks aria-hidden="true", causing screen readers to announce the integration list twice.
issuesFound.fix: Line: IntegrationsBar component, clone ul element: Add aria-hidden="true" to the ul element.

issuesFound.title: PlanCard CTA link missing unique accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The primary CTA link in the pricing card uses only the generic text from ctaLabel without including the plan name. Since multiple cards have similar CTAs, screen reader users cannot distinguish which plan each button corresponds to when navigating out of context.
issuesFound.fix: Line: PlanCard component, CTA anchor: Add aria-label={`${plan.ctaLabel} for ${plan.name} plan`} to the anchor props.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel non-visible slides missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Carousel slides that are not currently visible lack aria-hidden="true", allowing screen reader users to navigate to and read content from hidden slides, creating confusion about which testimonial is currently displayed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: TestimonialsCarousel component, slide div: Add aria-hidden={!isVisible} to the div props.
